decision.recommended_next_action: Use artifacts/wake_gate_integration/llama_cpp_queue/prepare_project_request.json and dispatch_request.json to enqueue the concrete autonomous llama.cpp validation run, or use the vLLM artifact if a heavier HF-server validation is desired.
decision.stop_reason: The integration branch met its success criterion by materializing wake-gate queue payloads and validating the copied harness against a real cached llama.cpp model backend with clean service shutdown.

run_notes_tail: # Wake-Gate Local Endpoint Harness Integration Run Notes
## Execution plan (2026-04-13)
1. Acquire the parent Local Model Endpoint Contract Harness artifacts into this branch workspace.
2. Inspect the queue/controller/wake-gate conventions available in nearby Enoch workspaces.
3. Wire a concrete autonomous-project integration artifact around the harness and a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint manifest.
4. Validate a real cached local model path when available; otherwise run the contract harness against a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible shim and record why a heavier backend was not selected.
5. Update durable evidence and `.omx/project_decision.json` before yielding.
## Branch-specific kill condition

Kill this branch if the copied harness cannot be executed against a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint after project-local dependency setup, or if no cached/constructible local endpoint can satisfy `/models` plus `/chat/completions` without credentials or destructive changes.
## Run results (2026-04-13)
- Acquired parent harness artifacts from `source-record-redacted`: `scripts/local_model_endpoint.py`, `tests/test_contract_harness.py`, manifests, README, and prior endpoint artifact.

- Added `scripts/build_wake_gate_integration.py`, which discovers cached HF snapshots and emits wake-gate `/prepare-project` + `/dispatch` JSON plus a materialized autonomous child project for endpoint validation.

- Created `artifacts/wake_gate_integration/20260413T090207Z/` with vLLM queue artifacts for cached `Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct` at `/home/jeremy/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--Qwen--Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct/snapshots/aa8e72537993ba99e69dfaafa59ed015b17504d1`.

- Created `artifacts/wake_gate_integration/llama_cpp_queue/` and `autonomous_llama_cpp_endpoint_project/` as the concrete quick autonomous wake-gate child run using cached GGUF `/mnt/usb/home/jeremy/.lmstudio/models/lmstudio-community/Phi-4-mini-instruct-GGUF/Phi-4-mini-instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf` and `/mnt/usb/home/jeremy/projects/llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-server`.
- Installed project-local vLLM/Transformers/Torch environment in `.venv` with `uv pip install --python .venv/bin/python vllm`; this makes the vLLM queue artifact actionable.

- Validated the parent harness against the real cached llama.cpp backend: `.omx/endpoints/wake-gate-llama-cpp-cached.endpoint.json` has `contract_probe.ok=true`; explicit reprobe saved to `artifacts/wake_gate_integration/manual/llama_cpp_probe.json` also has `ok=true` for `/models` and `/chat/completions`.
- Stopped the llama.cpp service; `artifacts/wake_gate_integration/manual/llama_cpp_status_after_stop.json` reports `alive=false`, so no helper server remains running from this project.
## Verification
- `python -m unittest tests/test_contract_harness.py` -> OK.
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q` -> `2 passed in 1.46s`.
- `.venv/bin/python -m compileall scripts tests` -> compiled successfully.
- Endpoint proof: `artifacts/wake_gate_integration/manual/llama_cpp_probe.json` contains `"ok": true`, `/models` status 200, and `/chat/completions` status 200.
## Conclusion

The branch hypothesis is supported: the local endpoint contract harness can be copied into an autonomous wake-gated project, represented as wake-gate queue payloads, and validated against a real cached model backend through llama.cpp. The heavier vLLM path is prepared and dependency-installed but not separately started because the branch requirement was satisfied by the accepted llama.cpp serving path.
# Wake-Gate Local Endpoint Harness Integration Run Notes
## Execution plan (2026-04-13)
1. Acquire the parent Local Model Endpoint Contract Harness artifacts into this branch workspace.
2. Inspect the queue/controller/wake-gate conventions available in nearby Enoch workspaces.
3. Wire a concrete autonomous-project integration artifact around the harness and a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint manifest.
4. Validate a real cached local model path when available; otherwise run the contract harness against a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible shim and record why a heavier backend was not selected.
5. Update durable evidence and `.omx/project_decision.json` before yielding.
## Branch-specific kill condition

Kill this branch if the copied harness cannot be executed against a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint after project-local dependency setup, or if no cached/constructible local endpoint can satisfy `/models` plus `/chat/completions` without credentials or destructive changes.
## Run results (2026-04-13)
- Acquired parent harness artifacts from `source-record-redacted`: `scripts/local_model_endpoint.py`, `tests/test_contract_harness.py`, manifests, README, and prior endpoint artifact.

- Added `scripts/build_wake_gate_integration.py`, which discovers cached HF snapshots and emits wake-gate `/prepare-project` + `/dispatch` JSON plus a materialized autonomous child project for endpoint validation.

- Created `artifacts/wake_gate_integration/20260413T090207Z/` with vLLM queue artifacts for cached `Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct` at `/home/jeremy/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--Qwen--Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct/snapshots/aa8e72537993ba99e69dfaafa59ed015b17504d1`.

- Created `artifacts/wake_gate_integration/llama_cpp_queue/` and `autonomous_llama_cpp_endpoint_project/` as the concrete quick autonomous wake-gate child run using cached GGUF `/mnt/usb/home/jeremy/.lmstudio/models/lmstudio-community/Phi-4-mini-instruct-GGUF/Phi-4-mini-instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf` and `/mnt/usb/home/jeremy/projects/llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-server`.
- Installed project-local vLLM/Transformers/Torch environment in `.venv` with `uv pip install --python .venv/bin/python vllm`; this makes the vLLM queue artifact actionable.

- Validated the parent harness against the real cached llama.cpp backend: `.omx/endpoints/wake-gate-llama-cpp-cached.endpoint.json` has `contract_probe.ok=true`; explicit reprobe saved to `artifacts/wake_gate_integration/manual/llama_cpp_probe.json` also has `ok=true` for `/models` and `/chat/completions`.
- Stopped the llama.cpp service; `artifacts/wake_gate_integration/manual/llama_cpp_status_after_stop.json` reports `alive=false`, so no helper server remains running from this project.
## Verification
- `python -m unittest tests/test_contract_harness.py` -> OK.
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q` -> `2 passed in 1.46s`.
- `.venv/bin/python -m compileall scripts tests` -> compiled successfully.
- Endpoint proof: `artifacts/wake_gate_integration/manual/llama_cpp_probe.json` contains `"ok": true`, `/models` status 200, and `/chat/completions` status 200.
## Conclusion

The branch hypothesis is supported: the local endpoint contract harness can be copied into an autonomous wake-gated project, represented as wake-gate queue payloads, and validated against a real cached model backend through llama.cpp. The heavier vLLM path is prepared and dependency-installed but not separately started because the branch requirement was satisfied by the accepted llama.cpp serving path.
